mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month acceleration in CPIAUCSL signals sticky inflation that erodes real corporate earnings expectations and tightens financial conditions, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns following CPIAUCSL acceleration are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE rises above 9.5%, elevated labor market slack triggers downward revisions to corporate revenue forecasts and compresses profit margins, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances when UNRATE exceeds 9.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in real retail sales (RSAFS) and industrial production (INDPRO) jointly signal a broad contraction in aggregate demand and supply chain destocking, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint declines in RSAFS and INDPRO do not precede negative 20-day returns with Rank IC greater than 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is held below 0.25% in a zero lower bound regime, month-over-month payroll growth (PAYEMS) drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days because marginal employment gains shift expectations toward early recovery.
failure_condition: PAYEMS increases during periods with FEDFUNDS below 0.25% fail to generate positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 45% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in HOUST below 650 thousand units signals collapsing residential investment and downstream demand for construction materials and consumer durables, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: HOUST readings below 650 are not followed by negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in at least 55% of observations over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When INDPRO declines while CPIAUCSL accelerates, stagflationary cost pressures simultaneously squeeze corporate margins and reduce real output, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint pattern of falling INDPRO and rising CPIAUCSL fails to produce negative 5-day forward returns with Rank IC above 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) reflect expanding household income and consumption capacity, which translate into positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive month-over-month changes in PAYEMS are followed by negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of observations over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month contraction in real retail sales (RSAFS) signals weakening consumer spending momentum that leads to negative earnings revisions and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Month-over-month declines in RSAFS are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of instances over a 3-year rolling window.
